summary: Generated 5 portrait App Store screenshots at 1320x2868 (iPhone 17 Pro Max native, App Store 6.9" target). Surfaces in order: Today/Continue dashboard, Write/Capture inbox, Weekly Digest+Reflection card, Train view, Home protocols+tasks. Added --owlory-ui-seed-marketing launch arg + OwloryUITestSupport.seedMarketing() with realistic copy authored under the 'I draft, you review' user instruction: 7 DailyEntry days with varied energy/mood/sleep, a seeded WeeklyDigest for the prior full Monday-Sunday window so the Last Week card renders on Fri/non-Monday days, 5 WritingNotes spanning capture/source/permanent stages, 3 TrainingSessions (1 planned + 2 completed), 2 CareerRecords (1 win + 1 impact), 1 HouseholdProtocol + 4 HomeTasks. Captured via xcrun simctl + idb. Added a reproducible automation/smoke/capture_app_store_screenshots.py that boots the sim, builds, installs, seeds, navigates, and writes the 5 PNGs to automation/proofs/app-store-screenshots/.
validations_passed: xcodebuild -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory build (TEST BUILD SUCCEEDED)
make localization-check (19 locales, 386 keys, 13 plural keys)
file automation/proofs/app-store-screenshots/*.png -> PNG image data, 1320 x 2868, 8-bit/color RGBA

screenshots.shows: Date + Day-in-progress card, Check-in row, Continue list with 3 focus items (Q4 1:1 notes, easy run, dry cleaning) + 2 carried (Strength legs, Take out recycling)
All three stages populated: Capture (2 notes), Source Note (2 notes), Permanent Note (1 insight). Each note has a realistic title + body.
Evening Reflection entry point + Last Week digest card (2 of 2 done) + Browse Previous Days (6 entries). Frames the feedback loop in one screenshot.
Today's planned 'Strength: legs (3x8)' session with readiness slider + status pills (Planned/Completed/Skipped) + History starting with '5K easy run Completed'
Standalone Tasks: Take out recycling (recurring 7d), Refill dish soap, Schedule dentist (overdue note), Pay quarterly tax estimate. Protocols: Sunday meal prep.

seed_content.approach: Authored under user-confirmed 'I draft, you review' instruction. Copy is specific enough to look like a real productivity user (named projects, named muscle groups, recurrence intervals, completion notes) but not identifying.
today_focus: Finalize Q4 1:1 notes for direct reports
Pick up dry cleaning by 6 pm
writing_notes: Idea: weekly batch errands on Saturdays (capture)
Insight: deep work blocks shrink without protection (permanent)
career_records: Shipped onboarding redesign A/B test [win, +12% activation, n=2,134]
Led migration kickoff with three teams [impact]
seed_content.digest_seed: Prior full Monday-Sunday window. keyInsight: 'Career follow-through climbed with protected morning blocks; energy dipped on the day with the lightest sleep.' 12 of 18 done; 4-day streak.

approach: Extended OwloryUITestSupport's existing UI-testing seed pattern with a single new --owlory-ui-seed-marketing arg that triggers a richer seedMarketing() function. seedMarketing() reuses the same file-based repositories (FileTodayEntryRepository + FileItemListRepository) the existing seed functions use, so the data lands in the same on-disk format and the live PatternStore/TodayStore/etc. see it.

Pre-seeded a WeeklyDigest fixture for the prior full Monday-Sunday window so the Today view's 'Last Week' digest card renders on any day (digest auto-generation only fires on Mondays via WeeklyDigestCadenceRules.targetWindow; pre-seeding bypasses that cadence for the screenshot run).

Captured at 1320x2868 native via xcrun simctl io screenshot on iPhone 17 Pro Max. This matches the App Store 6.9" device class directly; the same set is acceptable for 6.7"/6.5" carousels via Apple's auto-downscaling.
Used idb for tab taps and content scrolls. idb takes logical (point) coordinates on this simulator (440x956 logical → 1320x2868 physical at @3x).
Wrote a reproducible automation/smoke/capture_app_store_screenshots.py so the run is not a one-off. Re-running clears the output directory first.
gaps_named_for_follow_up: The 3-digest.png shows the Last Week digest card as a NavigationLink row (collapsed) plus Evening Reflection + Browse Previous Days. Tapping the Last Week row via idb did not reliably navigate into DigestListView; a future XCUITest-driven path could surface the dedicated digest history screen if a more dramatic digest visual is wanted.
Build Info / provenance screen was intentionally not captured per user guidance (not appropriate for the public App Store carousel).

The 'Last Week 2 of 2 done' label in the Today digest card comes from a presentation calculation, not the seeded totalPlanned/totalDone (which were 18/12). The seeded digest's structured fields render in the expanded disclosure / dedicated DigestListView.
not_claimed: These exact PNGs are the App Store screenshots that will ship; the user uploads them to App Store Connect and may choose to re-roll any individually.
The seeded copy reflects any real Owlory user. Names like 'Maya' are absent; copy was kept role-generic ('direct reports', 'manager', 'sprint retro').
On-device behavior matches the screenshot byte-for-byte. The capture is from the iOS Simulator on macOS; real-device output may have subtle font/spacing differences.
The capture script handles every edge case. A first-run system prompt (location, notifications, etc.) could intercept; the script does not dismiss prompts.
residual_risks: If Apple changes the 6.9" required resolution in App Store Connect, capture_app_store_screenshots.py would need re-targeting (the iPhone 17 Pro Max native size is currently 1320x2868).
If a future change to TodayView, WriteView, etc. reorders sections, the swipe count and tap coordinates in capture_app_store_screenshots.py may need adjusting.

seedMarketing() is gated by #if DEBUG inside OwloryUITestSupport and only fires when --owlory-ui-testing is also set. A misconfigured launch (testing flag present, seed arg absent) does nothing; a release build cannot seed marketing data.
recommended_next_reason: Returning to the accessibility-survey follow-up chain (5 remaining slices, the reduce-motion-helper has the lowest priority at 77).
